title: pochota coriacea subsp. orinocensis
intro: Pochota coriacea subsp. orinocensis, also known as the Orinoco Pochota, is a plant species within the family Sapindaceae, native to Venezuela. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Its health benefits include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as providing antioxidant and digestive support. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
